title: Dendranthema × cuneifolium
taxonomy: Dendranthema × cuneifolium is a cross between Dendranthema cuneifolium and possibly another species, which is part of the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is often classified as a hybrid of the species Dendranthema cuneifolium and Dendranthema grandiflorum. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and similarities to its parent species. The classification of Dendranthema × cuneifolium may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a hybrid daisy. The exact classification can be complex and is still a subject of study among botanists and horticulturists.
uses: Dendranthema × cuneifolium, also known as Chrysanthemum cinerariaefolium, has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in herbal remedies for its potential antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains a compound called pyrethrum, which has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems. Additionally, Dendranthema × cuneifolium has been used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. Its extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dendranthema × cuneifolium for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Dendranthema × cuneifolium, also known as Chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Chrysanthemum tea has been traditionally used to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections due to its antimicrobial properties. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids may have anti-diabetic and anti-hypertensive effects. Regular consumption of Chrysanthemum has been associated with improved immune function and reduced oxidative stress.
properties: Dendranthema × cuneifolium, commonly known as the Tansy Aster, is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential therapeutic uses. The plant's bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. Research suggests that extracts from Dendranthema × cuneifolium may have applications in treating various health conditions, including infections and inflammatory diseases. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic properties and potential benefits. The potential uses of Dendranthema × cuneifolium in traditional medicine and as a source of novel therapeutics warrant further investigation.
constituents: Dendranthema × cuneifolium, also known as Chrysanthemum balsamita or White Chrysanthemum, contains active constituents including flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and other phenolic compounds. These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and are often used in traditional herbal remedies. The sesquiterpenes present in the plant have been identified as the primary contributors to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Flavonoids, on the other hand, are known for their antioxidant properties. The specific types and proportions of these active constituents can vary depending on factors such as the plant's growing conditions and the specific variety of Dendranthema × cuneifolium.
parts: Dendranthema × cuneifolium, also known as White Chrysanthemum or White Shasta Daisy, is a plant with a long history of medicinal use in traditional Chinese medicine. The medicinal parts of Dendranthema × cuneifolium include the flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions such as fever, colds, and digestive issues. The flowers are particularly valued for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dendranthema × cuneifolium is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.

side_effects: Dendranthema × cuneifolium, also known as Silver Queen chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant that can be used in herbal medicine. However, there is limited information available about its side effects. Generally, consuming herbs and plants can cause allergic reactions, stomach upset, or interact with medications in some individuals. More research is needed to understand the potential side effects and risks associated with Dendranthema × cuneifolium. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can potentially exacerbate these issues, making it essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
intro: Dendranthema × cuneifolium, commonly known as Chrysanthemum morifolium or more specifically Dendranthema cuneifolium, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antiseptic to fight off infections. The bioactive constituents of Dendranthema × cuneifolium include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and caffeic acid derivatives, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea, extracts, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
